content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • •     Administration • •     Executive/Legal • •     Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          323-6441


September 18, 1984


Assemblyman Bruce C. Bronzan
State Capitol, Room 3147
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                          Re: Your Request for Advice,
                                              Our Advice No. A-84-217

Dear Assemblyman Bronzan:

     In your August 20, 1984, request for advice you raise a
number of novel and important questions regarding the Political
Reform Act which have not previously been addressed by the
Commission. We do not feel it is appropriate for the state to
resolve these issues without Commission guidance. Therefore, at
the next Commission meeting, we intend to ask the full Commission to issue a formal opinion in response to your questions.
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329(b); Government Code Section
83114(a). If the Commission agrees to issue an opinion, a
hearing on the questions you have raised will be scheduled,
probably in November or December. The procedures for such a
hearing are set forth in our regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18322 (copy enclosed).

     Pending the resolution of these issues by the full
Commission, I can only give you limited advice. First, the
Political Reform Act does not prohibit the Forum, or any other
group, from employing you or your staff members, or from paying
expenses for you or your staff.1/  The Political Reform Act
only imposes reporting or disqualification requirements on
public officials and committees. Since it is quite possible
that you, your committee staff, the Forum, and contributors to
the Forum could incur reporting responsibilities under the Act,
I suggest that you and the Forum both keep careful records of
all contributions to the Forum and all expenditures by the

_____________
1/ However, you should consult the Legislative Counsel as
to whether a staff member can be paid by the Forum for work
which the staff member is supposed to perform as a legislative
employee.

Assemblyman Bruce C. Bronzan
September 18, 1984
Page Two


Forum. In addition, if any employee of the Forum qualifies as
a lobbyist under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239 (copy enclosed)
both the lobbyist and the Forum will have to file with the
Secretary of State.

I am sorry that we cannot give you any more definite advice
at this time. However, we will encourage the Commission to
address the questions you raise as promptly as is possible.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM:km
Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: Assemblyman Bronzan asked whether the Political Reform Act prohibits a forum or group from employing him or his staff members, paying their expenses, and what reporting or disqualification requirements may apply to him, his committee, the forum, and its contributors.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a forum from employing the assemblyman or staff members or paying expenses, but it imposes reporting or disqualification requirements on public officials and committees. The FPPC will refer the novel questions to the full Commission for a formal opinion and hearing, likely in November or December, pending which only limited advice is provided regarding record-keeping and potential lobbyist filing requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an advice letter with reference number A-84-217. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers are present; question and conclusion information extracted from letter body. Letter indicates FPPC will defer definitive guidance pending full Commission hearing.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Assemblyman Bronzan asked whether the Political Reform Act prohibits a forum or group from employing him or his staff members, paying their expenses, and what reporting or disqualification requirements may apply to him, his committee, the forum, and its contributors.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a forum from employing the assemblyman or staff members or paying expenses, but it imposes reporting or disqualification requirements on public officials and committees. The FPPC will refer the novel questions to the full Commission for a formal opinion and hearing, likely in November or December, pending which only limited advice is provided regarding record-keeping and potential lobbyist filing requirements.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Assemblyman Bronzan that his questions regarding Political Reform Act implications of forum employment and funding raise novel issues requiring full Commission review and formal opinion, scheduled for hearing in November or December, with interim limited guidance on reporting requirements and record-keeping.
